# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees
### Regular Meeting — February 21, 2017

---

*These are unofficial notes based on an auto-generated transcript and may contain errors or omissions.*

---

## Opening
- Meeting called to order at 6:00 PM by President Bush.
- Quorum confirmed; meeting properly noticed under Texas Open Meetings Act.
- **Invocation:** Mr. Kydd
- **Pledges:** Mr. Hubert

---

## Special District Recognitions

### Oak Ridge High School Cheerleading Team — State & National Champions (UIL 6A)
- **Presented by:** Tommy Johnson, Principal of Oak Ridge High School
- **Head Coach:** Sarah Parker (teacher at Oak Ridge since 2006; head cheer coach for 7 years)
- The team won its **first-ever state championship** and followed up by winning **national championships** the following weekend, competing against 6A and 5A state winners.
- Coach Parker highlighted the team's achievements beyond competition:
  - All seniors have received college acceptance letters
  - Many are Honor Society members
  - Ranked in the **top 10 teams nationally** for fundraising for St. Jude Children's Hospital (sixth consecutive year; over $16,000 raised)
- Individual team members were introduced and recognized by the board.
- **President Bush** congratulated the team on behalf of the board, praising both their competitive achievement and community service.

### 2017 Texas Music Educators Association (TMEA) All-State Musicians
- **Presented by:** Dr. Horton, Coordinator of Fine Arts
- **24 students** from Conroe ISD were named 2017 TMEA All-State musicians.
- Dr. Horton explained the competitive process:
  - Begins with auditions across 33 TMEA regions statewide
  - Over 68,000 students compete; approximately 1,700 are selected (less than 3%)
  - Students advance through region and area competitions before earning All-State designation
- Notable achievement: **Brendan Wiley** was named **first chair clarinet** for the entire state of Texas.
- Students represented **Conroe High School, Oak Ridge High School, College Park High School, and The Woodlands High School** in choir, band, orchestra, and cello.
- Teachers from each campus were recognized and thanked.
- **President Bush** congratulated the students and teachers, emphasizing the importance of arts education and the growing movement from STEM to STEAM (adding Arts).

---

## Public Comment

### Christy Swoboda — Instructional Paraprofessional Pay
- Spoke about low pay for paraprofessionals in CISD.
- As a long-term substitute, she earned $100–$110/day; as a paraprofessional, she now earns less than $90/day.
- Her last paycheck was **$673.10**; after deductions, take-home was **$526.95**.
- Noted that if she enrolled in the district health and dental plan, she would clear only **$22.95** per pay period.
- Cited a colleague with **29 years of special education paraprofessional experience** who earns less than $25,000/year.
- Described the significant responsibilities paraprofessionals carry: RTI (Response to Intervention), data entry, small-group instruction, student testing, and supervision.
- Respectfully requested the board consider an **increase in paraprofessional pay**, acknowledging the difficult legislative environment.

---

## Action Items

### Item 4A — Director of Assessment, Evaluation, Accountability, and School Improvement (Moved Up on Agenda)
- **Moved up** before the consent agenda at the request of Mr. Luna.
- **Dr. Stockton recommended Dr. Tameka Taylor**, currently Principal of Travis Intermediate School.
- **Motion to approve:** Made and seconded.
- **Vote: Approved unanimously** (no discussion requested before vote).
- Dr. Taylor addressed the board, thanking them for their trust. She recounted her career in CISD since 2005, beginning at Runyon Elementary and serving at Travis Intermediate. She pledged to "leave a legacy, be tenacious, work hard, and embrace the enduring vision" of the district.
- President Bush personally acknowledged Dr. Taylor's impact, noting his own children benefited from her work.

### Consent Agenda
- **Item M removed** from the consent agenda at a board member's request.
- **Motion to approve consent agenda minus Item M:** Approved unanimously.

### Item M — Interlocal Agreement with City of Conroe for Flashing School Speed Limit Signs
- **Pulled by Mr. Hubert** for questions.
- **Cost to CISD: $40,000** for purchase of equipment; the City of Conroe handles installation and ongoing maintenance.
- **Dr. Heinz (Chris)** explained:
  - The city approached the district after the fiscal year started (not budgeted).
  - Flashing speed limit signs cost approximately **$11,000 per pair** (one for each direction).
  - These are **new installations**, not replacements.
  - Not every school in the district currently has flashing signs; there is no set criteria — placement depends on traffic studies, speed concerns, and cooperation with local entities (city, county precincts, TxDOT).
  - The district plans to return next year to continue expanding coverage.
  - Payment due within 30 days of approval; installation timeline not precisely known but expected "fairly quickly."
  - City Council has already approved the agreement.
- **Vote: Approved unanimously.**

### Item 5A — Construction Manager at Risk for Austin Elementary School Additions and Renovations
- **Presented by Mr. Foster.**
- Architect **IBI** published an RFQ; three companies responded.
- After a two-step evaluation process (qualifications + price/interviews), **GTT** was selected as best value.
- **Estimated project budget: $15 million.**
- Board authorized the superintendent to negotiate and execute the contract.
- **Vote: Approved unanimously.**

---

## Reports and Updates

### Item 5B — Capital Improvement Update (Mr. Foster)

| Project | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **High School Robotics Addition** | Behind schedule (rain/weather delays) | Interior expected ready by March; furniture delivery during spring break; student use likely after spring break |
| **Network Operations Center** | On schedule | Cooling units installed; full use expected summer 2017 |
| **Life Cycle 2017** | On schedule | Contractor mobilized; first roofing project starting end of February |
| **Knox Junior High** (10 classroom addition + fieldhouse work) | On schedule | Foundation work completed; opening August 2017 |
| **Woodlands Transportation Center** | On schedule | Demolition complete, reconstruction underway; opening August 2017 |
| **Safety & Security Project** | Phase 1 complete (16–18 campuses); Phase 2 bidding next week (~15 additional campuses) | Commissioning cameras and burglar panels |
| **Lucille Bradley Elementary** (new school) | On schedule | Opening August 2017; finishes going in; permanent power/AC expected within 30–45 days |
| **Grand Oaks High School** (new school) | On schedule | Opening August 2018; last beam set; building envelope work progressing |

### Item 6A — Financial Reports (Darrin Rice)

**General Fund:**
- Cash and investments: **$285 million** total
  - Pool investments: $160 million
  - Other investments (up to 1 year): $73.5 million
  - TCG Investment Advisors: $51 million
- Property tax collections running **5% ahead of prior year** (~$10 million received sooner), credited to the tax office's efforts including extended hours.
- Projected general fund balance increase: **~$5.1 million** (includes ~$3 million payroll reconciliation item from budget process).

**Debt Service:** Projected increase of ~$882,000.

**Child Nutrition:** Slight increase of ~$81,000.

**Self-Funded Insurance:**
- Positive trends: Total revenues of **$18.7 million** vs. total expenses of **$16.2 million** = **$2.5 million surplus**.
- At the same point last year, the fund was running about $500,000 in the negative (ultimately lost $2.5 million over the rest of the year).
- District still hoping to **break even** for the year; previous two years required supplemental funding.
- Wellness center visits: **2,211** year-to-date.

**Investments:**
- Total invested: **$616 million**
- Combined portfolio weighted average maturity (WAM): 61 days
- Combined yield: 91 basis points (vs. benchmark 1-year T-bill at 50 basis points)

---

## Adjournment
- Motion to adjourn: Made and seconded. **Approved.**
